Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: soc221b <22850435+soc221b@users.noreply.github.com>

Clean up tessl files and add symlink cleanup to sync-opencode.sh (#11)

* chore: stop tracking tessl-managed and .tessl/ files

- Add **/tessl__* and .tessl/ to .gitignore
- Untrack 45 tessl-managed files from all agent skill directories

* feat(scripts): add broken symlink cleanup to sync-opencode.sh

Removes broken symlinks under .agents/skills/ before syncing,
keeping the skills directory clean when tiles are removed.

* Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(scripts): address Copilot PR review comments in sync-opencode.sh

- Remove duplicate shebang: move shopt -s nullglob after set -euo pipefail
- Replace fragile grep/tr JSON parsing with jq for skill name extraction
- Use script-relative REPO_ROOT instead of $(pwd) for symlink targets so
  the script works correctly regardless of the working directory it is
  called from

* feat(cli): add `sync opencode` command to replace scripts/sync-opencode.sh

Ports the sync-opencode.sh shell script into the TypeScript CLI as
`tekhne sync opencode`. Implements the same three operations:

1. Remove broken symlinks from .agents/skills (or .opencode/skills)
2. Sync .mcp.json → opencode.json mcp section
3. Create/update tessl__ symlinks for all tiles in .tessl/tiles/

Supports --dry-run and --opencode-skills flags matching the original
script's behaviour. Adds sync:opencode and sync:opencode:dry-run npm scripts.

* refactor(cli): fix biome warnings in sync-opencode command

- Extract processTileSkillLink() helper to reduce cognitive complexity
  of syncTileSymlinks from 30 to within limit (15)
- Use template literal instead of string concatenation
- Prefix unused cwd param with underscore

* chore: update .gitignore to include dist/ directory

* docs(cli): document sync opencode command and remove replaced shell script

- Add Sync Commands section to cli/README.md
- Update Architecture section with sync.ts and cli/lib/sync/
- Add sync-opencode.sh → tekhne sync opencode to migration table
- Delete scripts/sync-opencode.sh (replaced by CLI command)

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Typography polish + natural apartment-number sort for unit/resident displays (#552)

* Initial plan

* Update admin unit form to use apartment number (Broj stana) instead of manual BM ID

- Add unitIdFromApartmentNumber() helper in app_main.js
- Change form label/placeholder to Broj stana in index.html
- Update setModeAdd/setModeEdit and save handler in app_admin.js
- Validate format (digits + optional letter), generate unitCode automatically
- Save both apartmentNumber and unitCode; backward-compat for legacy units

Co-authored-by: bgnikolapopovic-cloud <252382813+bgnikolapopovic-cloud@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add getUnitApartmentNumber() backward-compatible helper for BM-style unit ID parsing

- Parses apartment number from unitCode when apartmentNumber field is missing
- Handles all BM-style ID formats: BM25001A→1, BM25001AA→1A, BM25001AB→1B
- Returns "" as safe fallback; null-safe; stored value takes priority
- Smoke-test usage in setModeEdit() for legacy unit editing

Co-authored-by: bgnikolapopovic-cloud <252382813+bgnikolapopovic-cloud@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update unit UI: apartment number as primary label, BM code as muted secondary

- Add .unitAptLabel (17px bold) and .unitCodeMuted (10px muted) CSS classes
- Admin unit list: Stan br. X (primary) + BM code (muted secondary)
- Admin unit dropdown: Stan N — Owner instead of raw BM code
- Payment rows: Stan br. X + muted code in screen view (print unchanged)
- Ownership dialog: uses findUnitDB() to resolve full unit, shows apt label
- unitCardInline: Stan br. X primary + muted BM code in unit meta block
- Dashboard debtors: apartmentNumber propagated to summary records; Stan br. X shown
- Dashboard issues: Stan br. X derived from unitCode via shared helper

Co-authored-by: bgnikolapopovic-cloud <252382813+bgnikolapopovic-cloud@users.noreply.github.com>

* Typography polish: darker owner/resident names in unit displays

- .unitOwner: replace opacity:0.85/0.82 with color:rgba(15,23,42,.92)
- #page-finansije .unitRow .unitOwner: darken from 0.78 → 0.92
- .dashDebtItemOwner: 11px/no-weight/50% → 12px/w600/78% opacity

Co-authored-by: bgnikolapopovic-cloud <252382813+bgnikolapopovic-cloud@users.noreply.github.com>

* Sort all unit lists by apartment number (natural order 1, 1A, 2, 10, 10A)

- Add compareUnitsByApartmentNumber() in app_main.js after getUnitApartmentNumber
- app_admin.js: apply comparator in refreshParentOptions, redraw, user-asc dues sort
- messages.js: sort units before rendering compose dropdown

Co-authored-by: bgnikolapopovic-cloud <252382813+bgnikolapopovic-cloud@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: bgnikolapopovic-cloud <252382813+bgnikolapopovic-cloud@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Phase 20: UI polish — profile API, toast, skeletons, improved UX

Backend:
- Add PATCH /auth/profile (update name, phone, avatar)
- Add PATCH /auth/password (change password with current password verification)
- Add GET /vendors/:id public endpoint (vendor profile lookup)
- Enrich GET /auth/me to return phone, avatar, vendor info

Frontend:
- Install react-hot-toast, add Toaster to root layout
- Replace all alert() calls with toast notifications
- Create Skeleton component (ServiceCardSkeleton, TableRowSkeleton, ChatListSkeleton)
- Rewrite profile page: real API data, inline edit mode, save to API
- Rewrite settings page: password change form, logout button
- Add skeleton loaders to services list, admin users table, service detail
- Improve chat MessageBubble: rounded bubbles with shadow, white bg for received
- Improve ChatWindow: gray background, better empty state with icon
- Improve service detail: skeleton loading, sidebar with real images
- Improve notification dropdown: click navigates to relevant page
- Fix ChatButton/ContactButton: use proper toast import

commit.message:
Integration audit: fix upload auth, add admin list endpoints, wire mock pages

Backend fixes:
- Fix upload route roleGuard case: VENDOR/ADMIN → vendor/admin (was blocking all uploads)
- Fix upload controller response: status 'success' → 'ok' (consistency)
- Add GET /admin/users endpoint (list all users)
- Add GET /admin/vendors endpoint (list all vendors, optional status filter)
- Add getUsers() and getVendorsList() to admin service

Frontend fixes:
- admin/users page: replace hardcoded mock data with real API call
- admin/approvals page: replace mock data with real pending vendors/services + functional approve/reject buttons
- vendor.service.ts: getVendors() now calls /admin/vendors (was calling nonexistent /vendors route)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Phase 20: UX polish — filters, pagination, notifications, auth-aware navbar

- Services page: functional country/category/brand dropdown filters with pagination
- Navbar: show Dashboard link when authenticated, role-based routing
- NotificationBell: mark all read button, request/message links
- Vendor profile: polished layout with error states, gradient avatar
- Dashboard/Admin layouts: logout button, user name display
- Admin chats: use real auth store user ID instead of hardcoded
- Vendor service: mapVendor() normalizer for backend data

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: auto-append /api to base URL if missing

Prevents 404s when NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL is set to the root domain
without the /api path suffix.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add directUrl to prisma.config.ts for Neon advisory lock support

Prisma 7 uses prisma.config.ts (not schema.prisma) for URLs.
directUrl points to non-pooled Neon connection for migrations.
url remains the pooled connection for runtime queries.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use DIRECT_DATABASE_URL for migrations to avoid Neon pooler timeout

Neon's connection pooler doesn't support pg_advisory_lock needed by
Prisma migrate. Use direct (non-pooled) URL for migrations, fallback
to DATABASE_URL if not set.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: seed lookup data (categories, countries, brands) and demo services

4 categories, 4 countries, 4 brands, 2 demo services for vendor.
All use upsert/findFirst to avoid duplicates on re-run.

commit.message:
fix(supervisor): handle worker failure with lease reclaim and task recovery

Previously, the supervisor sent the terminal heartbeat (→ COMPLETING)
BEFORE checking the finalization result. COMPLETING is a terminal lease
state with no path to CRASHED or RECLAIMED, so failed workers left the
lease permanently stuck and the task stranded in ASSIGNED.

Now the supervisor checks the finalization outcome first:
- Success: sends terminal heartbeat → COMPLETING (unchanged)
- Failure: calls LeaseReclaimService to atomically transition the lease
  to CRASHED → RECLAIMED, evaluate retry/escalation policy, and recover
  the task (ASSIGNED → READY or FAILED based on retry budget)

The catch block (for workspace/runtime exceptions) also attempts lease
reclaim as best-effort recovery.

Adds LeaseReclaimerPort to WorkerSupervisorDependencies and
createReclaimUnitOfWork to the application adapters.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix mood palette puzzle: surface required tags for joyful carnival and creepy dungeon (#9)

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: leejsinclair <707675+leejsinclair@users.noreply.github.com>

docs: professional README for public repository

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: fallback through all LRC candidates on fetch failure

Previously, only the first synced candidate was tried. If its LRC file
was missing (404) or had invalid path chars like null bytes (400), the
entire repo was skipped.

Now iterates all candidates (synced first, preserving order) until one
succeeds. Skips paths containing null bytes. Only gives up when all
candidates fail.

Recovers ~24% of repos that were previously lost.

commit.message:
security: move SECRET_KEY to .env, gitignore backend_data and secrets

- Add .gitignore: exclude backend_data/, .env, node_modules, dist, __pycache__
- Add .env.example: public template with documented variables (SECRET_KEY,
  JWT_EXPIRE_HOURS, DATA_DIR, HOST, PORT)
- auth.py: read SECRET_KEY and JWT_EXPIRE_HOURS from .env via python-dotenv;
  remove file-based .jwt_secret (was committed to git — rotated)
- main.py: load .env at startup; resolve DATA_DIR from env var with fallback
  to ./backend_data and system paths; add python-dotenv import
- requirements.txt: add python-dotenv==1.0.0
- deployment/monitoring.service: add EnvironmentFile for .env loading;
  restore correct ExecStart with venv python path
- Purged backend_data/ (jwt_secret, services.json, layouts/) from entire
  git history using git-filter-repo

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(domain): add STARTING→COMPLETING lease transition for fast workers

Fast-completing workers (~1s) emit zero heartbeat events, so the lease
never reaches RUNNING. Previously this was worked around with a synthetic
'bridge' heartbeat that forced STARTING→RUNNING before the terminal
heartbeat. Three independent model critiques (Opus, GPT-5.4, Gemini 3)
identified this as a semantic lie and a staleness detection gap.

The real fix: add STARTING→COMPLETING as a valid state machine edge.
A worker that completes instantly genuinely skips RUNNING — the state
machine should reflect this reality. The terminal heartbeat still goes
through the heartbeat service (which sets heartbeatAt and validates the
transition), preserving staleness detection and audit trail integrity.

Removes the bridge heartbeat hack entirely.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: initial resonance lyrics ecosystem

- tools/: Rust crawler (crawl + build subcommands)
  - LRCHub GraphQL crawl with incremental early termination
  - 256-shard JSON output (SHA-256 based)
  - SQLite build + zstd compression
  - Language detection (ja/ko/zh/en)
  - Dirty shard tracking (only write changed files)

- .github/workflows/crawl.yml: daily incremental + weekly full
- .github/workflows/build.yml: SQLite + delta + Release
